title: neoporteria subgibbosa f. heteracantha
intro: Neoporteria subgibbosa f. heteracantha, also known as the "Queñua tree," is a type of tree native to the Andes mountains in South America, belonging to the genus Neoporteria and family Cactaceae. The medicinal properties of Neoporteria subgibbosa f. heteracantha include its ability to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its high content of fiber and antioxidants. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and even help with weight management. Traditional herbal preparations of Neoporteria subgibbosa f. heteracantha include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health issues and promote overall well-being.
